Analysis of False Belief Task (2)

Sean Trott

April 24, 2025

Load LLM data

# setwd("/Users/seantrott/Dropbox/UCSD/Research/NLMs/open_llm_tom/src/analysis")
directory_path <- "../../data/processed/fb2_local/"
csv_files <- list.files(path = directory_path, pattern = "*.csv", full.names = TRUE)
csv_list <- csv_files %>%
  map(~ read_csv(.))
## Rows: 64 Columns: 9
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

## chr (5): passage, knowledge_cue, condition, model_path, model_shorthand
## dbl (4): yes_prob, no_prob, item, log_odds

df_all_models <- bind_rows(csv_list) %>%
  mutate(model_shorthand = str_to_title(model_shorthand))
nrow(df_all_models)
## [1] 2368
length(unique(df_all_models$model_shorthand))
## [1] 37
table(df_all_models$model_path)

### Load #params
df_model_properties = read_csv("../../data/processed/model_properties.csv") %>% 
  mutate(model_shorthand = str_to_title(model_shorthand))
## Rows: 58 Columns: 5
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (4): model_path, model_shorthand, base_instruct, model_family
## dbl (1): num_params
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
nrow(df_model_properties)
## [1] 58
### Load training data
df_training_data = read_csv("../../data/processed/model_training_data.csv")%>% 
  mutate(model_shorthand = str_to_title(model_shorthand))
## Rows: 58 Columns: 4
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (3): model_path, model_shorthand, notes
## dbl (1): num_training_tokens
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
table(df_training_data$model_shorthand)
## 
##             Gemma 2 2b    Gemma 2 2b Instruct             Gemma 2 7b 
##                      1                      1                      1 
##    Gemma 2 7b Instruct            Llama 2 13b   Llama 2 13b Instruct

##                      1                      1                      1

### Join
df_all_properties = df_training_data %>%
  inner_join(df_model_properties)
## Joining with `by = join_by(model_path, model_shorthand)`
table(df_all_properties$model_shorthand)
## 
##             Gemma 2 2b    Gemma 2 2b Instruct             Gemma 2 7b

##                      1                      1                      1 
##   Qwen 2.5 7b Instruct 
##                      1
nrow(df_all_properties)
## [1] 58
#### Bind with FB data
df_all_models = df_all_models %>%
  inner_join(df_all_properties)
## Joining with `by = join_by(model_path, model_shorthand)`
nrow(df_all_models)
## [1] 2368
table(df_all_models$model_shorthand)
## 
##             Olmo 2 13b         Olmo 2 13b Dpo    Olmo 2 13b Instruct 
##                     64                     64                     64 
##         Olmo 2 13b Sft              Olmo 2 1b          Olmo 2 1b Dpo 
##                     64                     64                     64 
##     Olmo 2 1b Instruct          Olmo 2 1b Sft             Olmo 2 32b 
##                     64                     64                     64

  mutate(model_shorthand = str_to_title(model_shorthand))


length(unique(df_all_models$model_shorthand))

length(unique(df_all_models$model_family))

LLM Analysis

For the main comparison to human data, we focus only on the implicit items, i.e., where knowledge states aren’t directly stated.

Sensitivity to mental states

Each model on its own:

df_all_models %>%
  filter(knowledge_cue == "implicit") %>%
  mutate(condition = str_to_title(condition)) %>%
  filter(model_shorthand %in% c("Human",
                                "Olmo 2 32b Dpo",
                                "Olmo 2 32b Instruct",
                                "Olmo 2 32b Sft",
                                "Olmo 2 32b")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = log_odds,
             y = model_shorthand,
             fill = condition)) +
  geom_density_ridges2(aes(height = ..density..), 
                       color=NA, 
                       scale=.85, 
                       # size=1, 
                       alpha = .8,
                       stat="density") +
  labs(x = "Log Odds (Start vs. End)",
       y = "",
       fill = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom"
  ) + 
  theme(axis.title = element_text(size=rel(1.2)),
        axis.text = element_text(size = rel(1.2)),
        legend.text = element_text(size = rel(1.2)),
        legend.title = element_text(size = rel(1.2)),
        strip.text.x = element_text(size = rel(1.2))) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = viridisLite::viridis(2, option = "mako", 
                                                    begin = 0.8, end = 0.15))
## Warning: The dot-dot notation (`..density..`) was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
## ℹ Please use `after_stat(density)` instead.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.

# Function to fit models and perform model comparison
compare_models <- function(df) {
  if (n_distinct(df$condition) < 2) return(NULL)  # Skip if only one condition
  
  mod_full <- tryCatch(
    lmer(log_odds ~ condition +
           (1 | item), data = df, REML = FALSE),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  
  mod_reduced <- tryCatch(
      lmer(log_odds ~ 1 + (1 | item), data = df, REML = FALSE),
      error = function(e) NULL
  )
  
  if (is.null(mod_full) || is.null(mod_reduced)) return(NULL)
  
  anova_result <- anova(mod_full, mod_reduced)
  delta_aic <- AIC(mod_reduced) - AIC(mod_full)
  lrt_stat <- anova_result$Chisq[2]
  p_val <- anova_result$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2]
  
  # Extract coefficients
  coefs <- tryCatch(fixef(mod_full), error = function(e) return(rep(NA, 2)))
  cond_coef <- coefs[grep("^condition", names(coefs))]

  tibble(
    model_path = unique(df$model_path),
    delta_AIC = delta_aic,
    LRT_stat = lrt_stat,
    p_value = p_val,
    condition_coef = cond_coef
  )
}

# Apply to each model_path
results_by_model_path <- df_all_models %>%
  filter(knowledge_cue == "implicit") %>%
  group_by(model_path) %>%
  group_split() %>%
  map_dfr(compare_models)
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
results_by_model_path %>%
  arrange(LRT_stat)
## # A tibble: 37 × 5
##    model_path                      delta_AIC   LRT_stat p_value condition_coef
##    <chr>                               <dbl>      <dbl>   <dbl>          <dbl>
##  1 EleutherAI/pythia-160m              -2.00 0.00000583   0.998      -0.000154
##  2 EleutherAI/pythia-1b                -1.99 0.00640      0.936       0.00416 
##  3 EleutherAI/pythia-12b               -1.98 0.0177       0.894      -0.00803 
##  4 allenai/OLMo-2-0425-1B              -1.97 0.0309       0.860      -0.0126  
##  5 allenai/OLMo-2-0425-1B-DPO          -1.93 0.0662       0.797       0.0360  
##  6 EleutherAI/pythia-14m               -1.93 0.0681       0.794      -0.0155  
##  7 allenai/OLMo-2-0425-1B-Instruct     -1.81 0.195        0.659       0.0675  
##  8 EleutherAI/pythia-70m               -1.77 0.229        0.632       0.0166  
##  9 allenai/OLMo-2-0425-1B-SFT          -1.72 0.284        0.594       0.0517  
## 10 Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B                   -1.71 0.293        0.588       0.0420  
## # ℹ 27 more rows
summary(results_by_model_path$LRT_stat)
##      Min.   1st Qu.    Median      Mean   3rd Qu.      Max. 
## 5.800e-06 2.935e-01 4.540e+00 6.417e+00 1.131e+01 1.917e+01
summary(results_by_model_path$condition_coef)
##     Min.  1st Qu.   Median     Mean  3rd Qu.     Max. 
## -4.39785 -1.32777 -0.23032 -0.95033 -0.01551  0.06754
results_by_model_path$p_adj = p.adjust(results_by_model_path$p_value, method = "holm")
results_by_model_path = results_by_model_path %>%
  mutate(sig = p_adj < .05)
mean(results_by_model_path$sig)
## [1] 0.3783784
results_by_model_path %>%
  filter(sig == TRUE) %>%
  select(model_path, LRT_stat, p_adj, condition_coef)

All models together:

df_all_models_implicit = df_all_models %>%
  filter(knowledge_cue == "implicit")
### Mixed models
mod_full = lmer(data = df_all_models_implicit,
                log_odds ~ condition +
                  (1 + condition | model_path) + (1 + condition | item),
                REML = FALSE)

mod_reduced = lmer(data = df_all_models_implicit,
                log_odds ~ # knowledge_cue  + 
                  (1 + condition | model_path) + (1 + condition | item),
                REML = FALSE)

summary(mod_full)
## Linear mixed model fit by maximum likelihood . t-tests use Satterthwaite's
##   method [lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: 
## log_odds ~ condition + (1 + condition | model_path) + (1 + condition |  
##     item)
##    Data: df_all_models_implicit
## 
##       AIC       BIC    logLik -2*log(L)  df.resid 
##    4419.3    4465.0   -2200.7    4401.3      1175 
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -6.3495 -0.4779  0.0056  0.4283  5.2596 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups     Name             Variance Std.Dev. Corr 
##  model_path (Intercept)      3.2075   1.7909        
##             conditionunaware 1.4834   1.2180   -0.48
##  item       (Intercept)      0.4682   0.6842        
##             conditionunaware 0.3887   0.6235   -0.54
##  Residual                    1.9099   1.3820        
## Number of obs: 1184, groups:  model_path, 37; item, 16
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                  Estimate Std. Error      df t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)        1.2560     0.3452 49.1385   3.638 0.000658 ***
## conditionunaware  -0.9503     0.2662 41.6514  -3.571 0.000915 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr)
## conditinnwr -0.504
anova(mod_full, mod_reduced)
## Data: df_all_models_implicit
## Models:
## mod_reduced: log_odds ~ (1 + condition | model_path) + (1 + condition | item)
## mod_full: log_odds ~ condition + (1 + condition | model_path) + (1 + condition | item)
##             npar    AIC    BIC  logLik -2*log(L)  Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)

## mod_full       9 4419.3 4465.0 -2200.7    4401.3 11.125  1  0.0008519 ***

Accuracy metric

We first focus on the implicit items, since that’s what we have human data for.

df_all_models = df_all_models %>%
  mutate(correct = case_when(
    condition == "aware" & log_odds > 0 ~ TRUE,
    condition == "unaware" & log_odds <= 0 ~ TRUE,
    TRUE ~ FALSE  # all other cases are incorrect
  ))


df_summ = df_all_models %>%
  filter(knowledge_cue == "implicit") %>%
  group_by(model_path, model_shorthand, model_family,
           num_params, base_instruct, num_training_tokens) %>%
  summarise(mean_accuracy = mean(correct),
            n())
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'model_path', 'model_shorthand',
## 'model_family', 'num_params', 'base_instruct'. You can override using the
## `.groups` argument.
df_summ %>%
  select(model_shorthand, mean_accuracy) %>%
  arrange(-mean_accuracy) %>%
  head(5)
## Adding missing grouping variables: `model_path`, `model_family`, `num_params`,
## `base_instruct`
## # A tibble: 5 × 6
## # Groups:   model_path, model_shorthand, model_family, num_params,
## #   base_instruct [5]
##   model_path model_family num_params base_instruct model_shorthand mean_accuracy
##   <chr>      <chr>             <dbl> <chr>         <chr>                   <dbl>
## 1 allenai/O… Olmo            3.22e10 Instruct      Olmo 2 32b Dpo          0.812
## 2 allenai/O… Olmo            3.22e10 Instruct      Olmo 2 32b Ins…         0.781
## 3 allenai/O… Olmo            3.22e10 Instruct      Olmo 2 32b Sft          0.75 
## 4 Qwen/Qwen… Qwen 2.5        3.28e10 Instruct      Qwen 2.5 32b I…         0.719
## 5 Qwen/Qwen… Qwen 2.5        1.48e10 Instruct      Qwen 2.5 14b I…         0.688
mean(df_all_models$correct)
## [1] 0.6186655
df_summ %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_accuracy)) %>%
  select(model_shorthand, mean_accuracy) %>%
  head(5)
## # A tibble: 5 × 2
##   model_shorthand       mean_accuracy
##   <chr>                         <dbl>
## 1 Olmo 2 32b Dpo                0.812
## 2 Olmo 2 32b Instruct           0.781
## 3 Olmo 2 32b Sft                0.75 
## 4 Qwen 2.5 32b Instruct         0.719
## 5 Qwen 2.5 14b Instruct         0.688
### "Wisdom of the crowd"?
df_lo_avg = df_all_models %>%
  group_by(passage, condition) %>%
  summarise(m_lo = mean(log_odds)) %>%
  mutate(correct = case_when(
    condition == "aware" & m_lo > 0 ~ TRUE,
    condition == "unaware" & m_lo <= 0 ~ TRUE,
    TRUE ~ FALSE  # all other cases are incorrect
  ))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'passage'. You can override using the
## `.groups` argument.
mean(df_lo_avg$correct)
## [1] 0.7619048
df_summ %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = num_params,
             y = mean_accuracy,
             color = model_family,
             shape = base_instruct)) +
  geom_point(size = 6,
             alpha = .5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = .5, linetype = "dotted",
             size = 1.2, alpha = .5) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  # geom_text_repel(aes(label=model_shorthand), size=3) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
  labs(x = "Parameters",
       y = "Accuracy",
       color = "",
       shape = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_manual(values = viridisLite::viridis(8, option = "mako", 
                                                  begin = 0.8, end = 0.15)) +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 15),
        legend.position="bottom")

### Which properties predict mean accuracy?
summary(lm(data = df_summ,
           mean_accuracy ~ log10(num_params) +
             base_instruct + log10(num_training_tokens)))
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = mean_accuracy ~ log10(num_params) + base_instruct + 
##     log10(num_training_tokens), data = df_summ)
## 
## Residuals:
##       Min        1Q    Median        3Q       Max 
## -0.081389 -0.046035 -0.005032  0.023303  0.177731 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)                -0.519470   0.236842  -2.193 0.035437 *  
## log10(num_params)           0.074728   0.017231   4.337 0.000128 ***
## base_instructInstruct      -0.006569   0.024891  -0.264 0.793493    
## log10(num_training_tokens)  0.029389   0.019710   1.491 0.145435    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.06749 on 33 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.5082, Adjusted R-squared:  0.4635 
## F-statistic: 11.37 on 3 and 33 DF,  p-value: 2.814e-05
df_summ = df_all_models %>%
  filter(knowledge_cue == "implicit") %>%
  filter(item <= 8) %>%
  group_by(model_path, model_shorthand, model_family,
           num_params, base_instruct, num_training_tokens) %>%
  summarise(mean_accuracy = mean(correct),
            n())
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'model_path', 'model_shorthand',
## 'model_family', 'num_params', 'base_instruct'. You can override using the
## `.groups` argument.
df_summ %>%
  select(model_shorthand, mean_accuracy) %>%
  arrange(-mean_accuracy) %>%
  head(5)
## Adding missing grouping variables: `model_path`, `model_family`, `num_params`,
## `base_instruct`
## # A tibble: 5 × 6
## # Groups:   model_path, model_shorthand, model_family, num_params,
## #   base_instruct [5]
##   model_path model_family num_params base_instruct model_shorthand mean_accuracy
##   <chr>      <chr>             <dbl> <chr>         <chr>                   <dbl>
## 1 allenai/O… Olmo            3.22e10 Instruct      Olmo 2 32b Dpo          0.812
## 2 allenai/O… Olmo            3.22e10 Instruct      Olmo 2 32b Ins…         0.812
## 3 allenai/O… Olmo            3.22e10 Instruct      Olmo 2 32b Sft          0.812
## 4 Qwen/Qwen… Qwen 2.5        3.28e10 Instruct      Qwen 2.5 32b I…         0.75 
## 5 allenai/O… Olmo            1.37e10 Base          Olmo 2 13b              0.75
mean(df_all_models$correct)
## [1] 0.6186655
df_summ %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = num_params,
             y = mean_accuracy,
             color = model_family,
             shape = base_instruct)) +
  geom_point(size = 6,
             alpha = .5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = .82,
             linetype = "dotted", color = "red",
             size = 1.2, alpha = .8) + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = .5, linetype = "dotted",
             size = 1.2, alpha = .5) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  # geom_text_repel(aes(label=model_shorthand), size=3) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
  labs(x = "Parameters",
       y = "Accuracy",
       color = "",
       shape = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_manual(values = viridisLite::viridis(8, option = "mako", 
                                                  begin = 0.8, end = 0.15)) +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 15),
        legend.position="bottom")

Human analysis

df_human = read_csv("../../data/processed/human/exp1_aggregated.csv") 
## New names:
## Rows: 1135 Columns: 31
## ── Column specification
## ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Delimiter: "," chr
## (10): trial_type, condition, assignment, responses, stimType, categoriza... dbl
## (20): ...1, subject, rt, trial_index, time_elapsed, stimNum, numeric_cat... lgl
## (1): Congruent
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. ℹ
## Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
## • `` -> `...1`
df_human_critical = df_human %>%
  filter(exp_group == "Knowledge") %>%
  mutate(condition = case_when(
    condition == "Speaker Aware" ~ "aware",
    condition == "Speaker Unaware" ~ "unaware"
  )) %>%
  mutate(item = stimNum) %>%
  mutate(correct = case_when(
    condition == "aware" & categorization == "Yes" ~ TRUE,
    condition == "unaware" & categorization == "No" ~ TRUE,
    condition == "unaware" & categorization == "Yes" ~ FALSE,
    condition == "aware" & categorization == "No" ~ FALSE
  )) %>%
  select(subject, rt, categorization, correct, item, condition, rc.mean, exp.inf.mean)
  

df_human_critical <- df_human_critical %>%
  mutate(answer_binary = ifelse(categorization == "Yes", 1, 0))


llm_by_item <- df_all_models %>%
  filter(knowledge_cue == "implicit") %>%
  select(item, condition, log_odds, model_path, model_shorthand)

df_merged <- df_human_critical %>%
  inner_join(llm_by_item, by = c("item", "condition"))
## Warning in inner_join(., llm_by_item, by = c("item", "condition")): Detected an unexpected many-to-many relationship between `x` and `y`.
## ℹ Row 1 of `x` matches multiple rows in `y`.
## ℹ Row 3 of `y` matches multiple rows in `x`.
## ℹ If a many-to-many relationship is expected, set `relationship =
##   "many-to-many"` to silence this warning.

Now, fit a baselines model for each LLM.

fit_compare_glmer_by_model <- function(df) {
  if (n_distinct(df$answer_binary) < 2) return(NULL)  # skip degenerate cases

  # Fit full model
  mod_full <- tryCatch(
    glmer(answer_binary ~ condition + log_odds +
            (1 | item) + (1|subject),
          data = df,
          family = binomial()),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  # Fit reduced model (no condition)
  mod_reduced <- tryCatch(
    glmer(answer_binary ~ log_odds +
            (1 | item) + (1|subject),
          data = df,
          family = binomial()),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  if (is.null(mod_full) || is.null(mod_reduced)) return(NULL)

  # Model comparison (Likelihood Ratio Test)
  anova_result <- anova(mod_reduced, mod_full)
  lrt_stat <- anova_result$Chisq[2]
  p_val <- anova_result$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2]
  delta_aic <- AIC(mod_reduced) - AIC(mod_full)

  # Extract coefficient for log_odds
  log_odds_coef <- tryCatch({
    fixef(mod_full)["log_odds"]
  }, error = function(e) NA)

  tibble(
    model_shorthand = unique(df$model_shorthand),
    delta_AIC = delta_aic,
    LRT_stat = lrt_stat,
    p_value = p_val,
    log_odds_coef = log_odds_coef,
    condition_coef = fixef(mod_full)["conditionunaware"],
    
  )
}



# Apply across model_shorthand groups
glmer_model_comparisons <- df_merged %>%
  group_by(model_shorthand) %>%
  group_split() %>%
  map_dfr(fit_compare_glmer_by_model)
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
## Warning in checkConv(attr(opt, "derivs"), opt$par, ctrl = control$checkConv, :
## Model failed to converge with max|grad| = 0.00371044 (tol = 0.002, component 1)
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')

glmer_model_comparisons
## # A tibble: 37 × 6
##    model_shorthand     delta_AIC LRT_stat  p_value log_odds_coef condition_coef
##    <chr>                   <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>         <dbl>          <dbl>
##  1 Olmo 2 13b              186.     188.  8.06e-43        0.208           -2.84
##  2 Olmo 2 13b Dpo          100.     102.  4.56e-24        0.131           -2.74
##  3 Olmo 2 13b Instruct     104.     106.  8.95e-25        0.106           -2.75
##  4 Olmo 2 13b Sft          117.     119.  1.01e-27        0.284           -2.72
##  5 Olmo 2 1b               269.     271.  7.21e-61        0.190           -2.95
##  6 Olmo 2 1b Dpo           269.     271.  7.06e-61       -0.0830          -2.96
##  7 Olmo 2 1b Instruct      269.     271.  6.30e-61       -0.0362          -2.96
##  8 Olmo 2 1b Sft           261.     263.  4.30e-59       -0.312           -2.95
##  9 Olmo 2 32b              113.     115.  8.51e-27        0.207           -2.71
## 10 Olmo 2 32b Dpo           71.9     73.9 8.36e-18        0.109           -2.52
## # ℹ 27 more rows
summary(glmer_model_comparisons$log_odds_coef)
##     Min.  1st Qu.   Median     Mean  3rd Qu.     Max. 
## -0.31192  0.02215  0.13068  0.15843  0.28404  0.64125
summary(glmer_model_comparisons$condition_coef)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##  -3.076  -2.958  -2.932  -2.860  -2.840  -2.509
summary(glmer_model_comparisons$LRT_stat)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##   72.71  135.20  254.60  202.38  270.90  274.81

Compare effects:

#### Do this for condition
# Calculate mean Log Odds for each model andcondition
df_all_models$answer = df_all_models$log_odds > 0

df_by_condition <- df_all_models %>%
  group_by(model_path, condition) %>%
  summarise(mean_yes = mean(answer),
            .groups = "drop")

# Calculate overall mean across all LMs
df_overall_mean <- df_all_models %>%
  group_by(condition) %>%
  summarise(mean_yes = mean(answer),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(model_path = "All LMs Mean")

df_human_mean = df_human_critical %>%
  filter(rc.mean >= 9.5) %>%
  group_by(condition) %>%
  summarise(mean_yes = mean(answer_binary),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(model_path = "Human")




# Add labels to the data
set.seed(1)
df_overall_mean <- df_overall_mean %>% mutate(label = "LLM Mean")
df_human_mean <- df_human_mean %>% mutate(label = "Human")
ggplot(df_by_condition, aes(x = condition, y = mean_yes)) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, alpha = 0.3, size = 2, color = "gray40") +
  geom_point(data = df_overall_mean, 
             aes(x = condition, y = mean_yes),
             color = "#1f77b4", size = 5, shape = 18) +
  geom_text_repel(data = df_overall_mean,
                  aes(x = condition, y = mean_yes, label = label),
                  size = 4, color = "#1f77b4") +
  geom_point(data = df_human_mean, 
             aes(x = condition, y = mean_yes),
             color = "#d62728", size = 5, shape = 17) +
  geom_text_repel(data = df_human_mean,
                  aes(x = condition, y = mean_yes, label = label),
                  size = 4, color = "#d62728") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Knowledge State",
       y = "Proportion of Yes Responses") +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 15))

# Calculate human difference
df_human_diff <- df_human_mean %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = condition, values_from = mean_yes) %>%
  mutate(diff = `aware` - `unaware`)

human_diff <- df_human_diff$diff
# Calculate the difference for each model
df_cond_diff <- df_by_condition %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = condition, values_from = mean_yes) %>%
  mutate(diff = `aware` - `unaware`)  # Adjust these names to match your levels

# Calculate mean difference
mean_diff <- mean(df_cond_diff$diff)

# Plot the distribution of differences
ggplot(df_cond_diff, aes(x = diff)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 20, alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_density(color = "#1f77b4", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean_diff, color = "#1f77b4", linewidth = 1.5, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = human_diff, color = "#d62728", linewidth = 1.5, linetype = "solid") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "black", linetype = "dotted") +
  annotate("text", x = mean_diff, y = Inf, label = "LLM Mean",
           hjust = -0.1, vjust = 1.5, color = "#1f77b4", size = 4) +
  annotate("text", x = human_diff, y = Inf, label = "Human",
           hjust = 1.1, vjust = 3, color = "#d62728", size = 4) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Difference in Proportion 'Yes' Responses (Aware - Unaware)",
       y = "Density") +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 15))

Restrict human accuracy by reading comprehension, etc.

summary(df_human_critical$rc.mean)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##   0.000   8.000   9.500   8.668  10.000  10.000
summary(df_human_critical$exp.inf.mean)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##    1.50    5.00    7.50    7.16    9.00   15.50
df_human_critical %>% 
  filter(rc.mean >= 9.5) %>%
  summarise(mean_accuracy = mean(correct))
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##   mean_accuracy
##           <dbl>
## 1         0.838
df_human_critical %>% 
  filter(exp.inf.mean >= 7.5) %>%
  summarise(mean_accuracy = mean(correct))
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##   mean_accuracy
##           <dbl>
## 1         0.827
